Hi, I'm Matthew Dex, and I'm a storyteller. You are too. I'm the type of storyteller who stands on stages and speaks to hundreds and sometimes thousands of people at a time. Maybe you're that kind of storyteller, but probably not. But you're definitely a storyteller. You probably tell stories to your friends and your loved ones, your coworkers, a police officer pulls you over, you're trying to tell a story to get out of a ticket. Almost everyone in the world is a storyteller of some kind. But maybe you're a hermit. Maybe you're living in some cave in Northern Canada and somehow you've managed to wire in some electricity, and you're watching this video huddled in your cave, cold and wet and lonely with no intention of ever speaking to another human being ever again. Even if that is you, you are a storyteller. I fundamentally believe that the most important person we tell stories to is ourselves. I think the most important audience you have ever had in your entire life for the stories you tell is yourself. I think that once we start to recognize that stories are for other people, but first and most important, they're for ourselves, I think our lives can actually get better. I think that when we begin to look at our lives through the narrative of storytelling, and specifically we start to find the stories in our lives that we fail to see, fail to hold on to, fail to capture and make meaning of, when we start to fix all that, when we start to recognize those stories, hold on to them and tell them to ourselves, we can start to feel better and more whole. Our lives can feel richer and larger and more complete. The purpose of this course is to help you find stories. Even if you never want to tell a story to another person in the world, you should be actively, aggressively, relentlessly looking for stories in your life to tell yourself. You are the most important audience you will ever have for your stories. As you start to collect stories in the ways that I'm going to teach you over the course of the next several lessons, you're going to discover that you lead a life that you never understood before. You're going to discover that your life is filled with stories. You're going to discover that the more stories you start to see, collect and hold on to, the better you're going to feel about the life you're living. The more excited you're going to feel about the life you're living. When you start to look at narrative as a function of your life, when you start to see your life as a movie, as a collection of stories that are comprising an enormous tale that will ultimately be the course of your life, when you start to see all of this, you'll start to be a happier person. We're here to collect stories. We're here to find them, hold on to them and speak them, maybe to other people, but first to ourselves. But my promise to you is this, at the end of this course, you will have many strategies to find new stories in your life. By the end of this course, you will have more stories to tell than you've ever imagined before. When you look back on the course of your life and you look at the days that you're living today, you'll discover that you're living a more extraordinary life than you've ever imagined before. I'm going to teach you five strategies to help you both find the stories that you're living today, the ones that you're experiencing on a day-to-day basis, as well as look back and recapture the stories that you've left behind. My goal is for you to have a mountain of stories at the end of this course. More stories than you've ever imagined you had before. I want you to find those stories so that you can tell them to yourself and maybe to other people. But here's my promise to you and it's a ridiculous one, but it's true. Once you find these stories, once you start seeing them and collecting them, you're going to be a happier person. That's a crazy, ridiculous promise to make, but it is true. I want you to know that I'm not a unicorn in any way whatsoever. I don't live a more interesting life than anyone. I don't meet celebrities or travel the world. I'm a pretty ordinary guy who has committed himself to finding stories in his life and piling them up. My purposes are many. I stand on stages and I tell my friends stories at the golf course. I tell my family stories at the dinner table. I tell my fifth grade students stories constantly, but the most important person I tell stories to is myself and I'm happier for it. Ten of thousands of people all over the world are doing this with me. So I don't want you to think that your life is bereft of stories. I don't want you to think that you're boring or ordinary or that your life is so mundane that this won't work for you. You're just not that special. You're just like the rest of us. You live a life filled with stories and you've simply failed to see them, recognize them for what they are and hold on to them. I'm going to teach you how to do all of that. Five strategies that will change your life and make you a happier person, even if you never tell a single story to another human being for the rest of your life. So let's get started.
